article_title: Unitree Clears Shanghai STAR Market IPO Review, Billed as China's First Embodied-AI Listing
summary: Every financial figure verified against the cited outlet; one procedural detail ('registration, issuance and pricing') attributed to The Robot Report is not in that source's snapshot — filed as a clarification.

findings.message: The clause 'As The Robot Report notes, clearing the listing-committee review still leaves registration, issuance and pricing ahead before shares trade' attributes a specific procedural enumeration to The Robot Report, but the words 'registration', 'issuance', and 'pricing' do not appear in that source's snapshot.
findings.details: Recoverable: The Robot Report's thesis ('the humanoid case is still early') does support the broader 'approval is procedural, not final' point, and the remaining-terms-unsettled point is independently correct (echoed in the article's own 'What We Don't Know' and by Global Times' 'issuance, listing' notice). The specific three-step enumeration is the article's framing of standard China-IPO process, not the source's wording. Filed as a clarification.

content_preview.title: Unitree Clears Shanghai STAR Market IPO Review, Billed as China's First Embodied-AI Listing
content_preview.summary: The Shanghai Stock Exchange's STAR Market listing committee cleared the humanoid robot maker's IPO on Monday, a key hurdle toward a 4.2-billion-yuan raise.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Humanoid robot maker Unitree Robotics has cleared a major hurdle toward a public listing in Shanghai. According to the [South China Morning Post](https://www.scmp.com/tech/big-tech/article/3355538/unitree-clears-key-hurdle-shanghai-ipo-chinas-humanoid-robot-wave-gathers-pace), the Shanghai Stock Exchange's STAR Market listing committee cleared the Hangzhou-based company's initial public offering on Monday, one of the most closely watched offerings in China's embodied-intelligence se...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong, well-sourced business News piece (628 words) on Unitree's STAR Market listing-committee clearance, careful to mark it as a procedural milestone and to flag cross-outlet differences in the profit figures.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 6 snapshots read from disk (HTTP 200). source-0 (SCMP) confirms the Monday listing-committee hearing, the Hangzhou base, the March 20 Star Market filing, the 4.2 billion yuan (~$621M) raise, the two-rounds-plus-on-site review, 2025 revenue of 1.7 billion yuan, the 590.8 million yuan net profit, and the Dobot/ChiNext/Leju/Deep Robotics peer moves. source-1 (Rest of World) confirms the 'world's largest humanoid robot maker' headline quote, 5,500 robots sold in 2025, revenue 1.71B yuan ($250M) vs 392M yuan ($57M), the 600M yuan ($90M) adjusted net profit, the 593,400→167,600 yuan ($85,000→$25,000) price drop, and the UBTech/Optimus/100-company backdrop. source-2 (The Robot Report) confirms the 3,701-produced / 3,551-sold nine-month figures and the proceeds allocation. source-3 (CGTN) confirms the 'embodied artificial intelligence' A-share framing; source-4 (Global Times) confirms the SSE 'issuance, listing' notice; source-5 (TechNode) confirms the 73-day timeline and the Meituan 9.65% stake.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every financial figure, percentage, share count, and price traces to the specific outlet it is cited to, with disciplined attribution and the cross-outlet profit discrepancy (590.8M vs 600M) transparently flagged in 'What We Don't Know'. One procedural detail: the 'registration, issuance and pricing' steps attributed to The Robot Report are not in that source's snapshot — the broader 'still procedural/early' point is supported by it, but the specific enumeration is the article's own framing. Filed as a clarification. The 'STAR Market' styling matches SCMP's 'Star Market' (same board). The internal cross-reference to the June 3 Isaac GR00T article was verified to exist.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional; appropriately attributes the 'first embodied-AI listing' framing to state broadcaster CGTN ('Billed as…') rather than asserting it, and hedges the unsettled offering terms.
editor_notes.originality: New event (the IPO listing-committee clearance), distinct from the archive's GD01-mecha and Isaac GR00T Unitree coverage, the latter cross-referenced.
concerns: One procedural enumeration imprecisely attributed to The Robot Report — filed as a clarification; the underlying point is accurate.
recommendations: Added technode.com (independent China-tech outlet) and news.cgtn.com / globaltimes.cn (Chinese state/state-affiliated outlets, used for attributed framing only, consistent with the already-listed xinhuanet/tass/rt).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively accurate and fully financially verified. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS for the single imprecise procedural attribution.
